news.headline: Gold +1.59% to $4,191/oz on weak US jobs data; Silver +2.65% — Fed rate hike bets scale back
news.what_happened: Weaker-than-expected US employment data prompted markets to scale back Federal Reserve rate hike bets. Gold climbed toward $4,200/oz, its highest level in recent weeks. Silver surged 2.65% to $62.69/oz, a structural supply-deficit metal with industrial demand support.
news.why_it_matters: Gold price rise directly raises collateral values for gold-loan NBFCs (MUTHOOTFIN, MANAPPURAM), boosting AUM and fresh-disbursal capacity. Broader signal: Fed dovish pivot expectations support EM inflows into India. Risk-off tone favours defensives over cyclicals today.

news.headline: Hang Seng +1.12% | China NBS PMI 50.3 in June — 3rd consecutive expansion, new orders rebound
news.what_happened: Hang Seng rose 1.12% Monday. China's NBS Manufacturing PMI came in at 50.3 for June (vs 50.0 prior, est 50.1). New orders returned to expansion at 51.2 (vs 49.9). Foreign export orders also recovered to 50.1 from 48.6. Output accelerated to 51.4. This is the third consecutive month of factory expansion.
news.why_it_matters: China is the world's largest steel and aluminium consumer. Three months of PMI expansion with new orders and export orders recovering = tangible demand pick-up for Indian metals companies. Direct P&L positive for TATASTEEL, HINDALCO, JSWSTEEL.

news.headline: Nasdaq -0.80% as tech stocks decline; US markets reopen after July 4 holiday long weekend
news.what_happened: US markets reopened Monday after the July 4 Independence Day holiday (markets closed Thursday-Friday). Nasdaq fell 0.80%; S&P 500 was essentially flat. Tech stocks led the decline even as the broader market held up.
news.why_it_matters: Nasdaq weakness is a direct near-term sentiment headwind for Indian IT sector. However, USD/INR at 95.20 (a 52-week high for USD) provides structural revenue-conversion tailwind for IT exporters.

news.headline: RBI holds repo rate at 5.25% in June MPC; India-US interim trade deal talks target July 24 deadline
news.what_happened: RBI's June MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, cutting its GDP growth forecast to 6.6%. Separately, India and the US are in high-level talks for an interim trade deal to be finalised before July 24, targeting zero-duty market access for select sectors.
news.why_it_matters: Rate hold is neutral for banks/NBFCs near-term. The US-India trade deal, if concluded, is a significant positive catalyst for pharma (SUNPHARMA, DRREDDY) and IT exporters (TCS, INFY) who would benefit from tariff relief.

news.headline: FII net buyers ₹1,355 Cr on July 5 | DII net sellers ₹1,954 Cr — Nifty rises 4th consecutive week
news.what_happened: Foreign institutional investors were net buyers of ₹1,355 Cr in cash segment on July 5. DIIs were net sellers of ₹1,954 Cr. Indian markets closed at their highest level in multi-weeks, with Nifty above 24,270, supported by IT, pharma, realty, and metal stocks.
news.why_it_matters: FII buying continuation is a floor for large-caps. DII selling at these levels may create intraday supply. The 4-week Nifty rally means some profit-booking risk near highs.

picks.catalyst: Gold +1.59% to $4,191/oz — weak US jobs data triggered gold rally; Silver also +2.65%; J.P.Morgan $6,000 year-end target intact
picks.logic: Muthoot Finance's entire business is gold-backed lending (~90% portfolio). When gold prices rise: (1) existing loan LTV ratios improve giving headroom for top-ups, (2) new borrowers can pledge smaller quantities for same loan amount, lifting disbursal volumes, (3) AUM trajectory re-rates upward. Every sustained 1% rise in gold = multi-quarter AUM and NIM tailwind. Stock has strong recent momentum and gold at $4,191 nearing $4,200 is a psychological bullish level.

picks.catalyst: Hang Seng +1.12% + China PMI 50.3 — 3rd consecutive expansion with new export orders recovering to 50.1
picks.logic: Hindalco is India's largest aluminium and copper producer with global operations via Novelis. China is the world's largest aluminium consumer (~60% of global demand). The critical signal today is China's new export orders rebounding from contraction (48.6) to expansion (50.1) — this is the forward-looking indicator that directly foreshadows demand for Hindalco's products. Three consecutive months of PMI expansion reduces China hard-landing fears.

picks.catalyst: Hang Seng +1.12% + China PMI new orders sub-index 51.2 (vs 49.9 prior) — first expansion in 2 months
picks.logic: Tata Steel is an integrated steel producer in India and Europe. Steel demand tracks industrial activity and construction. China's PMI new orders sub-index jumping from 49.9 (contraction) to 51.2 (expansion) signals demand recovery in the world's largest steel market. Combined with Hang Seng rally confirming improved China risk sentiment, global steel price direction is positive. Tata Steel's India operations benefit from domestic demand, while its European operations gain from global pricing.

risks: GIFT Nifty signals ~220-point gap-down open — reduce position size at open; wait for price to stabilize before entering
Nasdaq -0.80% is risk-off signal; if US pre-market selling deepens before Indian market open, metals picks can reverse quickly
MUTHOOTFIN may gap up significantly at open due to gold move — verify watch zone is reachable; do not chase above ₹3,100
USD/INR at ₹95.20 (near 52-week high) is persistent macro risk — no intraday trigger today but structural import-cost burden
US-India interim trade deal deadline July 24 — uncertainty caps near-term upside for pharma/IT export plays
